title: catalpa bignonioides f. aurea
intro: Catalpa bignonioides f. aurea, also known as the golden catalpa or golden Indian bean, is a deciduous tree cultivar of the catalpa species, characterized by its yellow or golden foliage and large, heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of various alkaloids and saponins. Some of the health benefits associated with catalpa include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and even exhibit antiviral properties. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of these uses.
